title: Exciting News: Samba Rock Team Joins Chameleon Collective

content.text: I have some exciting news to share. But first, let me take you back to where this story began.

In 2009, I moved from London to Miami after two years at Sapient. I was assigned to work with a new creative agency called iChameleon, which had just begun collaborating with Sapient Miami. That's where I met Freddie Laker and joined an incredible group of thinkers, many of whom are now part of Chameleon Collective. We worked together for four transformative years that deeply shaped how I view creativity, leadership, and collaboration.

Fast forward to 2015. I took the leap into entrepreneurship and launched Samba Rock. Around that same time, Freddie and his partners launched Chameleon Collective. Our paths ran parallel: two similar visions built on collaboration and the belief that the traditional agency model was ready for reinvention.

Recently, Freddie and I reconnected, and one conversation stood out. It wasn't about joining his companyâit was about joining a collective of entrepreneurial minds. A group of senior leaders united by a shared purpose: helping brands grow through creativity, strategy, and transformation.
After some reflection, I realized this was the opportunity I'd been waiting forâa chance to do what I love, but on a broader, more collaborative stage.

Chameleon Collective isn't just another agency. It's a collective of extraordinary people across every discipline, from branding and marketing to commerce and technology, working as one to help businesses evolve and succeed.

Samba Rock will always be a part of meâit's shaped who I am and what I believe in. And now, Samba Rock is also part of Chameleon Collective, which means its spirit, its values, and everything we built together lives on in an even bigger way.
I'm grateful for this opportunity and excited for what's aheadânot just for me, but for what we can accomplish together.
The next act begins now. Together.
Samba Rock Joins Chameleon Collective, Expanding Creative and Strategic Capabilities Across Latin America

Chameleon Collective today announced that Samba Rock, a creative and strategic consultancy with deep experience across Latin America, will be joining its network of industry-leading experts. This integration strengthens Chameleon Collective's ability to help brands grow through creative innovation, digital transformation, and culturally informed strategy.

Samba Rock Founder and CEO Valter Klug will join Chameleon Collective as Marketing Leader, International Brand Expansion, bringing more than 25 years of experience leading creative and digital strategy for some of the world's most iconic brands, including Microsoft, Coca-Cola, Intel, Sony PlayStation, AT&T, and Reckitt Benckiser.

With extensive experience working with brands from Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Costa Rica, Colombia, Cuba, and Mexico, and more, Klug has built a reputation for blending creativity and strategic precision.

title: Why Companies Are Abandoning $25,000 Quarterly Focus Groups

content.text: The market research industry has a dirty secret: traditional methodologies are failing modern businesses

Last month, a Fortune 500 consumer goods company spent $180,000 on focus groups over six months to test campaign variations for a seasonal product launch. By the time they received their final insights, the season had passed, market conditions had shifted twice, and their competitors had already launched three similar products.
This isn't an isolated incident. It's become the norm.

After 25 years in global market intelligence, working with brands such as AB-InBev, Microsoft, Nokia, Coca-Cola, AT&T, and Intel, I've witnessed a troubling pattern: companies are spending more on market research than ever before, yet making slower and less confident decisions about their customers.
The $25,000 Quarterly Ritual

Walk into any marketing department at a mid-to-large company, and you'll find the same quarterly ritual. Teams allocate $15,000 to $25,000 for focus groups, then wait 6-8 weeks for insights that might be corrupted by group dynamics, moderator bias, or artificial conference room environments.
Meanwhile, their competitors are testing concepts, iterating messaging, and optimizing products in daysânot weeks.
The Speed vs. Quality Dilemma

The traditional market research playbook made sense in the 1940s when it was invented. Gathering people physically was necessary, expert moderators were rare, and analysis took weeks because everything was manual.
But forcing 1940s methodology onto 2025 business speed creates fundamental problems:

Time Mismatch: Digital campaigns launch in days. Product features ship weekly. Competitive responses happen overnight. Waiting 6-8 weeks for customer feedback means testing concepts in a market that no longer exists.

Geographic Limitations: Testing a national campaign with a focus group in Miami doesn't represent the entire country. Running multiple groups across regions multiplies costs and extends timelines even further.
The Observer Effect: Customers behave differently when they know they're being watched in sterile conference rooms with cameras and one-way mirrors.
The Bias Problem No One Discusses
Here's what the research industry doesn't want to admit: traditional focus groups systematically suppress authentic customer insights through group dynamics.

Social psychology studies consistently show that 20-30% of focus group participants dominate 60-70% of discussion time. Quieter voicesâoften your most thoughtful customersâget drowned out entirely.
The Technology Solution That Actually Works

The companies gaining a competitive advantage aren't abandoning customer researchâthey're using human-supervised AI methodologies that eliminate traditional research sins while preserving insight quality.

This isn't about replacing human judgment with algorithms. It's about using technology to eliminate recruitment nightmares, scheduling conflicts, geographic limitations, and group dynamics bias while keeping human expertise where it matters most.
The Competitive Advantage Window
The companies adopting these methodologies first are gaining a 5-7 week competitive advantage on every product launch, campaign test, and strategic decision.
The Path Forward
Market research isn't broken because companies don't value customer insights. It's broken because the methodologies haven't evolved with business speed and technological capabilities.

The question isn't whether AI-assisted research will replace traditional focus groups. The question is whether your team will adapt now and gain a competitive advantage, or spend another quarter committing the same costly methodological mistakes while competitors move faster.
Which company will yours be?

title: The Growth Intelligence Revolution: Why Smart Businesses Are Trading Long Consulting Cycles for AI-Powered Insights

content.text: How the convergence of artificial intelligence and strategic expertise is democratizing growth intelligence for ambitious brands worldwide

For nearly a decade, I've watched ambitious brands struggle with the same growth paradox: they know they need strategic intelligence to break through revenue plateaus, but traditional consulting timelines don't match market speed. By the time most firms deliver their recommendations, the opportunity has shiftedâor worse, the competition has moved first.

This disconnect between strategic need and delivery speed isn't just frustrating; it's economically dangerous. In today's market velocity, waiting six months for a growth strategy is like using a road map from 2019 to navigate 2025 traffic patterns.
The Traditional Consulting Bottleneck

The consulting industry has operated on the same fundamental model for decades: extensive discovery phases, comprehensive analysis, and detailed recommendations delivered in presentation format. This worked when markets moved slowly and competitive advantages lasted years, not quarters.

But today's business environment demands different intelligence. When a Miami restaurant chain needs to understand Jacksonville expansion potential, or when a Brazilian beauty brand is evaluating Target's latest vendor requirements, waiting months for strategic clarity means missing the window entirely.
Enter Growth Intelligence: The AI-Strategy Convergence

At Samba Rock, we've spent the past year developing what we call "Growth Intelligence"âa methodology that combines artificial intelligence with strategic expertise to deliver actionable market insights in weeks, not months.

This isn't about replacing human strategic thinking with algorithms. It's about using AI to accelerate the data gathering and pattern recognition phases, allowing experienced strategists to focus on interpretation, synthesis, and recommendation development.
The result: clients receive decision-grade intelligence that would traditionally take 12-16 weeks in just 14 days.
Why This Matters Now: The Democratization of Strategic Intelligence

This acceleration isn't just about speedâit's about access. Previously, comprehensive market intelligence was primarily available to large corporations with substantial consulting budgets. Small and mid-sized businesses often made expansion decisions based on instinct and limited data.

Growth Intelligence democratizes strategic insight. A $2M revenue service business can now access the same caliber of market analysis as a Fortune 500 company, at a fraction of the traditional cost and timeline.
Cultural Intelligence as Competitive Advantage
One unexpected insight from our international expansion work has proven crucial for domestic growth: cultural intelligence creates strategic advantages even within familiar markets.

A restaurant chain expanding from Miami to Atlanta isn't just changing locationsâthey're navigating cultural differences in dining preferences, price sensitivity, and brand positioning. Growth Intelligence incorporates these cultural insights alongside traditional market analysis.
The Rhythm of Modern Growth

After over 25 years in strategic consulting and brand development, including over a decade building Samba Rock, I've learned that sustainable growth has a rhythm. It's not about moving faster than everyone elseâit's about moving at the right speed for your market opportunity.

Growth Intelligence doesn't replace strategic thinkingâit accelerates it. In a world where competitive advantages are increasingly temporary, the ability to make informed decisions quickly becomes the ultimate sustainable advantage.
The question isn't whether your business needs better strategic intelligence. The question is whether you'll access it at market speed or consulting speed.

In todayâs digital landscape, businesses must harness the power of digital marketing services, including content marketing, search engine optimization (SEO), and Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO), to stay competitive. The challenge is not just attracting potential customers but fostering long-term growth through actionable insights and strategic insights.

Many business owners find themselves overwhelmed by the complexities of managing digital marketing while also focusing on core business objectives. Whether your brand is struggling with rising customer acquisition costs or needs to optimize customer retention, leveraging a specialized growth marketing agency can provide the support needed to reach your growth goals.
To do so, you first need to understand what your main business pains are. Here we selected the top 4, based on our decades of experience in the field:
1. Youâre Stretched Thin Trying to Manage Everything

For brands at any stage of growth, managing marketing tasks can be a distraction from the key drivers of business growth. Many executives try to handle their digital presence internally, only to realize that their marketing teams lack the expertise required to navigate a complex digital platform. This is particularly challenging for international brands adapting to the U.S. market, or U.S.-based brands that need a fresh growth strategy to break through stagnation.

The Role of a Growth Marketing Agency: A growth marketing agency serves as an extension of your team, providing data-driven strategies that allow you to focus on your core business. With the right agency partner, such as a Digital growth agency like Samba Rock, businesses gain access to a range of specialized services, from Google Ads management to content creation and digital campaign execution. This holistic approach allows companies to engage their customer base more effectively, ultimately achieving positive ROI and exponential growth.
2. Your Marketing Results Arenât Driving Sales

In todayâs fast-paced market, simply running digital ads or posting on social media wonât guarantee results. Many brands struggle with digital strategies that fail to deliver measurable improvements in conversion rates or customer engagement. Without an effective strategy in place, these efforts can fall flat, leaving brands wondering how to turn their online presence into real business results.

How Data-Driven Strategies Drive Revenue Growth: Growth marketing companies specialize in crafting growth marketing campaigns based on deep analysis of digital customer behavior. Using tools like Google Analytics, growth marketing experts can track the entire customer journey, optimizing each touchpoint for higher conversion rates and better customer loyalty. This process, known as conversion rate optimization (CRO), ensures that every dollar spent on digital marketing contributes to revenue growth.

By implementing a growth hacking strategy, businesses can test and refine digital marketing services that work best for their audience, driving incremental gains in market share and customer retention. These growth experimentsâfocused on tactics such as Inbound marketing, SEO, and social media marketingâare key to achieving sustainable growth in a competitive market.
3. Scaling Your E-Commerce Feels Overwhelming

For both international brands entering the U.S. market and domestic brands seeking rapid growth, managing e-commerce in todayâs digital landscape can be daunting. From retail marketplaces like Amazon to fully-managed marketplace strategies, navigating these platforms requires a deep understanding of user experience, online experiences, and how to engage with ideal customers.

E-Commerce Solutions for Rapid Growth: A specialized growth marketing partner can help brands develop a digital strategy that optimizes major sales channels. By focusing on conversion rate optimization, customer engagement, and organic traffic growth, growth agencies create a seamless digital customer experience that drives sales. This approach to digital growth also helps businesses increase their website traffic, improve their search engine rankings, and ultimately achieve long-term growth.

By leveraging innovative solutions like Growth Media and advanced digital growth strategies, growth marketing agencies can provide a comprehensive approach to scaling e-commerce operations. Whether your brand is new to the U.S. market or simply looking to optimize its existing presence, having a growth agency that understands the nuances of customer relationships in the digital space is crucial.
4. Hiring an In-House Marketing Team Is Too Expensive

One of the most common challenges for growing businesses is the high cost of building and managing an internal marketing team. Salaries, benefits, and training quickly add up, while internal teams often lack the specialized skills necessary for driving business growth in todayâs digital landscape.

Outsourcing for Cost-Efficient Growth: Outsourcing your digital marketing and e-commerce operations to a growth marketing agency can provide access to a wide range of expertise at a fraction of the cost of building an in-house team. Growth marketing experts handle everything from digital marketing campaigns to content creation, ensuring your brandâs growth journey is supported by actionable data and effective growth marketing strategies. With a focus on metrics like conversion rates and customer retention, these agencies help you achieve your growth objectives without the overhead.

Partnering with a growth agency that has a proven track record in executing successful campaigns also allows you to benefit from their extensive experience in digital growth. Whether your goal is to increase your market share or optimize your online presence, a growth marketing partner can provide the strategic insights necessary to guide your brand to success.
Why Growth Marketing Is Essential for Long-Term Success

Achieving sustainable growth in todayâs competitive environment requires more than traditional tactics. By working with a growth marketing agency, businesses can leverage data-driven insights, innovative solutions, and a holistic approach to digital marketing that drives measurable results. Whether youâre looking for rapid growth or planning for the long-term, having a growth marketing team that understands the complexities of the customer journey is essential.

In addition, growth marketing companies can help you adapt to changing market trends, providing valuable insights that keep your growth curve on the rise. By utilizing a combination of SEO, content marketing, conversion rate optimization, and targeted digital advertising, brands can build customer loyalty while reducing customer acquisition costs.
Conclusion: Unlocking Exponential Growth in a Competitive Market

Whether youâre entering the U.S. market as an international brand or facing growth challenges as a U.S.-based business, partnering with a growth marketing agency can help you unlock exponential growth. By focusing on data-driven strategies, creative program management, and a holistic approach to digital growth, your brand can achieve its growth goals while navigating a competitive landscape.

From optimizing your Google Ads campaigns to increasing organic traffic, growth agencies provide the tools, expertise, and innovative approaches necessary to drive your brandâs success. With a focus on delivering positive ROI and revenue growth, these agencies help you stay ahead of the curve and turn your growth opportunities into measurable business results.

In todayâs fast-paced digital world, achieving business growth requires more than just traditional marketing. By embracing the power of growth marketing and aligning your strategies with data-driven decisions, you can unlock your brandâs full potential and scale for long-term success. And if you ever need a hand, find out how Samba Rock can help with your business pains in our website. We are eager to help!

} Open Menu Close Menu .top-bun, .patty, .bottom-bun { height: 1px; } Open Menu Close Menu MENU ð§ð· HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ MENU ðºð¸ HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call Folder: MENU ð§ð· Back HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ Folder: MENU ðºð¸ Back HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call AI in Advertising: The Superpower You Didnât Know You Needed (But Keep the Cape On) Aug 19 Written By Valter Klug Here at Samba Rock, we've been riding the Artificial Intelligence wave, and let me tell you, it's been a total game-changer for our advertising efforts. Need to extract insights from a mountain of customer behavior and campaign performance data? AI's got it covered, transforming raw numbers into actionable insights faster than you can say "machine learning algorithm." It's like having a marketing AI superhero on your team, tirelessly crunching data and uncovering hidden gems of Competitive Intelligence.But hold your horses, folks! AI is no replacement for the human touch. It's a powerful tool, like a super-efficient assistant who never sleeps, optimizing your advertising processes and content strategy without breaking a sweat. Need to proofread, generate eye-catching image assets, or whip up a soundtrack for those engaging social media posts? AI's got you covered. But the true magic, the soul-stirring art of marketing, comes from you.AI may generate the beats, but you're the DJ, spinning the tracks into client-wowing masterpieces that boost conversion rates and leave your target audience wanting more. It's your intuition, strategic thinking, and the spark of human creativity that elevate AI's capabilities, crafting AI-generated marketing campaigns that resonate with niche audiences and even audience lookalikes.Samba Rock's AI Dance Moves:Data Insights & Research: Like a marketing Sherlock Holmes, AI unearths golden nuggets of customer behavior and campaign performance data from the vast landscape of big data. But it's your marketing team, armed with their degrees in Marketing, that polishes those nuggets into valuable, customer experience-focused, advertising strategies.

Organizing Chaos: AI brings order to the chaos, structuring data and summarizing meeting notes faster than a speed-reading champion. But crafting compelling narratives from this information? That's where your marketing genius shines.

Creative Assistance: From generating captivating image creatives to composing jingles and animating visuals, AI is the ultimate creative sidekick. It handles the narrow, repetitive tasks so you can focus on the big picture, crafting advertising examples that dazzle and inspire.

Polishing Content: AI proofreads and refines copy like a meticulous editor, particularly for non-native speakers. But adding that final flourish, that human touch that captivates readers? That's all you, wordsmith!

Efficiency Boosters: Like a turbocharged assistant, AI skims through documents, extracts key points, and tackles manual tasks like OCR with lightning speed. It's your secret weapon for conquering those time-consuming chores and freeing up your valuable time for strategic thinking.

We wrote this emotional brand manifesto for Saphirus Air Fresheners. This manifesto was 100% written by humans. Then, we leveraged ElevenLabs Generative Voice AI to create a voice-over of this manifesto, and even though it sounds human, the tempo of the delivery was a little off. So we edited it manually and added a music soundtrack that matched the feeling, and our creative team selected video clips that went well with the mood of this piece. So basically AI was only used for the voiceover, and everything else was human-made.

Last year, to celebrate the Holidays, we created this emotional video-holiday-card to send to our clients, team, friends and families. All the video clips originated as Midjourney images, animated using Runway Gen-2. The music is human-made, and the clips were edited by a real editor to fit the mood of the song. We had to create around 500 images, and about 100 animations, to get to this finished product through a lot of trial and error.

In conjunction with the recent rebranding of Samba Rock, and to demonstrate our passion for innovation and technology, we produced an AI-generated album based on the agencyâs manifesto. The video was released on July 13th, which is the International Day of Rock. The album, which was created and produced by Samba Rock, can be found on all major streaming platforms. Each song is in a different musical genre, and is named after a sentence of our brand manifesto, so you can read the entire manifesto by reading the tracklist.

AI is a transformative force, not a magic wand. Itâs an invaluable asset in your marketing efforts, capable of automating manual tasks, optimizing campaign effectiveness, and even generating captivating video ads through Generative AI. But it won't replace the human element that makes your work unforgettable.

Embrace AI as your strategic partner, a tool to amplify your brilliance. Use it to handle the mundane, freeing you to focus on the heart of marketing: crafting big ideas, forging strong customer relationships, understanding customer questions, and delivering exceptional customer service.
The AI-Powered Future of Advertising

In this current landscape of modern advertising, the fusion of AI and human creativity is the key to success. As AI continues to evolve, shaping the future of advertising with innovations like Performance Max, Google Shopping Ads, and Google's AI (Gemini), it's crucial to stay ahead of the curve. Embrace the generative AI process, harness the power of AI-generated advertising, and leverage tools like sentiment analysis to create advertising campaigns that resonate with your target audience.

Remember, while AI may be the cape, you're the superhero. You're the one who transforms AI's raw potential into marketing gold. So, let AI handle the heavy lifting while you focus on what truly matters: connecting with your audience and creating marketing content that leaves a lasting impression.

The harmonious dance of AI and human ingenuity is the future of marketing. It's a partnership that fosters customer satisfaction, drives customer retention, and maximizes your advertising budgets. So keep exploring, keep creating, and keep pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the thrilling world of digital marketing.
As the curtain falls on this Artificial Intelligence-infused marketing performance, remember: the spotlight is on you.
Till next time, keep the creativity flowing!

Marketing leaders in this field must leverage a variety of strategies to capture consumer attention and drive loyalty. Here, we explore key marketing tactics and trends crucial for success in the competitive market of household products, focusing on North America.
Understanding the Target Audience

A deep understanding of the target audience is fundamental to effective CPG marketing. Marketing specialists employ audience intelligence tools and analytics to gain insights into consumer behavior. These tools help in identifying individual consumer preferences, which are critical for crafting personalized shopping experiences. By analyzing data-driven insights, companies can tailor their marketing efforts to meet the needs and desires of potential customers.
Effective Marketing Strategies
Social Media Marketing and Influencer Marketing

Social media platforms are indispensable in todayâs marketing strategies. Brands like Procter & Gamble and Kraft Heinz utilize social media advertising and influencer marketing to reach broader audiences and create engaging content. Social media campaigns and social media ads are effective in generating user-generated content, which serves as social proof and enhances consumer trust.
Email Marketing and Loyalty Programs

Email marketing remains a powerful tool for nurturing relationships with loyal customers. Coupled with well-designed loyalty programs, it can significantly boost customer retention and long-term loyalty. These initiatives encourage repeat purchases and foster a sense of community among consumers.
Content Marketing and SEO

Content marketing is another vital component, where the focus is on creating valuable, educational content that resonates with the target audience. An effective content strategy, combined with search engine optimization (SEO), enhances the brandâs digital presence and drives organic search traffic. This approach is particularly important for establishing authority and trust in a saturated market.
Data-Driven Marketing and Analytics

Data-driven marketing strategies enable brands to make informed decisions based on consumer insights. By leveraging first-party data and advanced analytics tools, marketing teams can optimize their campaigns, improve targeting, and measure the effectiveness of their marketing spend. This approach is crucial for adapting to market trends and shifts in consumer behavior.
Adapting to Market Trends

An omnichannel strategy is essential for providing a seamless shopping experience across various marketing channels. This includes integrating digital platforms with physical stores to ensure a consistent brand experience. For example, consumers can research products online and make purchases in brick-and-mortar stores or vice versa. This strategy caters to the shift to online shopping and the need for a seamless omnichannel experience.
Mobile Marketing and Online Advertising

With the rise of mobile devices, mobile marketing and online advertising have become pivotal. Effective campaigns on digital channels, including display advertising and app advertising, are crucial for reaching consumers on the go. These tactics help brands capture consumer attention and drive digital purchases.
Commitment to Sustainability

In recent economic times, there is a growing emphasis on sustainability initiatives. Brands are increasingly focusing on sustainable packaging, eco-friendly products, and ethical practices to appeal to conscious consumers. This commitment to sustainability not only attracts new customers but also strengthens loyalty among existing ones.
Navigating Fierce Competition
Innovative Approaches and Product Development

In a crowded market, innovation is key. Companies must continuously invest in product development to offer unique selling propositions that differentiate them from competitors. This includes creating special packaging, introducing new product lines, and enhancing product quality.
Market Research and Consumer Insights

Regular market research and consumer insights are essential for staying ahead of the competition. By understanding consumer preferences and purchasing decisions, brands can adapt their marketing strategies to meet evolving demands. This involves monitoring market trends, analyzing competitive market dynamics, and responding to shifts in consumer behavior.
Building a Strong Digital Presence

A strong digital presence is crucial for CPG brands. This includes maintaining an active presence on social channels, optimizing online channels, and engaging in community building. By leveraging digital marketing strategies, brands can connect with consumers, build relationships, and drive growth.
Retail Stores and Market Share

Despite the rise of online shopping, retail stores remain a vital channel for CPG brands. Maintaining a strong presence in grocery stores and other physical locations is crucial for capturing market share and meeting consumers where they are. Effective in-store marketing campaigns, such as promotional displays and special packaging, can enhance visibility and drive sales.
Marketing Campaigns and Initiatives

Marketing campaigns and initiatives are the backbone of CPG marketing efforts. These can range from advertising campaigns across various channels to innovative growth marketing tactics to expand the customer base. Well-planned marketing initiatives, based on marketing trends, ensure that brands stay top-of-mind for consumers, even in a competitive market.

Samba Rock Marketing Consulting specializes in helping CPG brands navigate this complex marketing landscape. Our agency offers comprehensive marketing solutions tailored to the unique needs of each brand, leveraging data-driven insights, innovative digital marketing strategies, and a deep understanding of consumer behavior. We excel in crafting effective marketing plans, executing multi-channel marketing campaigns, and driving consumer loyalty through targeted marketing initiatives. Visit this page to learn more about how we can help your brand achieve significant growth and success in the competitive CPG market.

CPG marketing is a dynamic field that requires a multifaceted approach. By combining traditional marketing tactics with innovative strategies, brands can navigate the complexities of a competitive market. From social media marketing and email marketing to data-driven insights and sustainability initiatives, the key to success lies in understanding the target audience and adapting to ever-changing consumer behaviors. As the marketing landscape continues to evolve, CPG brands must remain agile, data-informed, and consumer-focused to achieve long-term loyalty and growth.

title: From Layoff to Launch: My Journey from Y&R Miami to Samba Rock

content.text: 0 Skip to Content @supports (-webkit-backdrop-filter: none) or (backdrop-filter: none) { .header-blur-background { } } MENU ð§ð· HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ MENU ðºð¸ HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call .top-bun, .patty, .bottom-bun { height: 1px; } Open Menu Close Menu .top-bun, .patty, .bottom-bun { height: 1px; } Open Menu Close Menu MENU ð§ð· HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ MENU ðºð¸ HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call Folder: MENU ð§ð· Back HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ Folder: MENU ðºð¸ Back HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call From Layoff to Launch: My Journey from Y&R Miami to Samba Rock May 29 Written By Valter Klug In 2015, I faced a turning point in my career that many might consider a setback but turned out to be a catalyst for a new beginning. After a fruitful tenure at Y&R Miami (Young and Rubicam, coming from a recent merge with Wunderman Thompson and Bravo), a leading advertising agency and part of the largest communications services group, WPP Group, my journey took an unexpected turn. This is how a layoff became an opportunity to build Samba Rock Marketing Consulting and start achieving my dream of making advertising history.

At Y&R Miami, I was a Creative Director, leading digital, social media, and art direction for major accounts like AT&T and Bauducco. My role involved developing marketing strategies that resonated with diverse audiences, particularly targeting the Hispanic market. It was an incredible experience to be part of an advertising agency with such a rich history, founded by Raymond Rubicam, and known for its innovative marketing strategies and impactful advertising services.

Y&R Advertising, now part of VML, under the umbrella of WPP Group, is recognized as one of the advertising giants. With an impressive client list, including the likes of AT&T, Coca-Cola, and Pepsico, Y&R's influence in the advertising industry is undeniable. This multinational advertising division, with its principal offices located in Columbus Circle, has a legacy that continues to shape the landscape of advertising and marketing today.

In early 2015, Y&R underwent significant restructuring like many multinational advertising divisions under the WPP Group, after losing their largest client at the time, AT&T. With the impact on their annual revenue, they saw the need to temporarily reduce the company size, resulting in layoffs that affected many, including myself. It was a moment of uncertainty, but also one of reflection. I realized that my (then) 16 years of experience in integrated advertising, interactive design, and social media, coupled with my work across three continents, had equipped me with a unique skill set and a dynamic network of connections in the industry.
Making Lemonade: The Birth of Samba Rock

Inspired by the challenges I faced and driven by a desire to create something of my own, I founded the international advertising agency Samba Rock Marketing Consulting in March 2015. An American company based in Miami with a subsidiary in SÃ£o Paulo, Samba Rock is a marketing communications company dedicated to helping brands expand globally, with a special focus on the American market. Our mission is to bridge cultural gaps and connect brands with consumers through effective advertising & marketing strategies.

Starting Samba Rock from scratch was no small feat - ownership in advertising agencies is one of the hardest jobs. With no investors and relying solely on my expertise and determination, I built the agency into a thriving business. Today, we collaborate with renowned brands such as The Nature Conservancy, AB-Inbev (GuaranÃ¡ Antarctica), Conchita Foods, and Bauducco Foods, among others. Our work spans various forms of communications, from advertising services to multicultural marketing, and we pride ourselves on our ability to adapt and innovate in a rapidly changing market.

Our agency, while young, aims to make a mark among the established public relations firms and boutique agencies created by advertising veterans. By integrating consumer insights and fostering affinity among consumers, we help brands develop a strong connection with their target audience. This approach ensures long-term brand performance and success.
Reflecting on the Journey

Looking back, my time at Y&R Advertising, one of the biggest ad firms in the world, and part of the WPP Group, one of the largest communications networks, was instrumental in shaping my career. The experience taught me the importance of resilience and adaptability. It also reinforced the value of creativity and strategic thinking in building long-term brand performance and fostering connections for brands.

At Samba Rock, we continue to leverage these lessons, offering a comprehensive suite of advertising, branding, e-commerce, and marketing services to a broader client portfolio. Our team, comprised of advertising veterans, is dedicated to delivering exceptional results and driving growth for our impressive client list by building a strong connection with consumers.

Our commitment to innovation and excellence is reflected in our membership in the Forbes Business Council and our collaboration with renowned public companies and healthcare networks. We aim to be a leader in the dynamic network of communications companies, continually expanding our client base and enhancing our service offerings.

As we move forward, Samba Rock remains committed to pushing the boundaries of what a marketing communications company can achieve. We strive to be at the forefront of innovation, helping brands navigate the complexities of global expansion and connect with consumers in meaningful ways. We aim to be the international advertising agency of record for the best brands in the world.

The journey from Y&R Miami to Samba Rock has been one of transformation and growth. It is a testament to the power of turning challenges into opportunities and the enduring impact of creativity and passion in the world of advertising and marketing.
Read the article I wrote for Forbes about this life experience by clicking this external link.
For more insights and updates on our work, visit Samba Rock Marketing Consulting or connect with me on LinkedIn.

Competitive insights provide actionable intelligence that allows businesses to stay ahead of key competitors and adapt to market shifts. This article explores how to leverage competitive insights to enhance your marketing strategies, using SEO keywords for maximum impact.The Importance of Competitive InsightsCompetitive insights are essential for making informed decisions and strategic planning. By analyzing the actions, strengths, and weaknesses of key competitors, businesses can develop more effective marketing strategies and improve their market positioning. Competitive intelligence helps in identifying market gaps and understanding customer preferences, enabling businesses to tailor their products and services to better meet the needs of their target markets.Conducting Competitive AnalysisEffective competitive analysis involves a thorough examination of the competitive landscape. This process includes gathering data on market leaders, understanding their marketing activities, and evaluating their online presence. Competitor analysis tools and competitive analysis reports provide detailed insights into competitor activity, allowing businesses to benchmark their performance and identify areas for improvement.Steps for Competitive Analysis:Identify Key Competitors: List the biggest companies and individual companies within your industry. Focus on those that pose the biggest threat to your market share.

Analyze Online Presence: Examine the social media profiles, content strategy, and engagement rate of your competitors. This includes monitoring social media platforms, microblogging platforms, and image- and video-sharing platforms.
Evaluate Marketing Campaigns: Look at the marketing strategies and marketing campaigns of your competitors. Pay attention to inbound marketing campaigns and digital marketing channels.
Gather Customer Feedback: Analyze customer reviews and feedback on competitor products. This provides insights into customer experience and customer preferences.
Use Competitive Analysis Tools: Utilize tools for competitor analysis to gather meaningful insights and compile a comprehensive competitive analysis report.
Leveraging Competitive Insights
Once you have gathered detailed insights into your competitors, itâs time to leverage this information for strategic decisions. Here are some ways to utilize competitive insights:
1. Improve Marketing Strategies:
Use competitor insights to refine your marketing approaches and marketing efforts. Understand what works for your competitors and adapt those strategies to fit your business model.
Develop a social media strategy that increases social media engagement and enhances your online presence.
2. Enhance Product Offerings:
Conduct a competitive product analysis to identify opportunities for product differentiation. Focus on feature design and feature rollouts that offer unique benefits to customers.
Use customer feedback and competitor benchmarking to improve individual product performance.
3. Optimize Customer Experience:
Improve customer service and customer support based on insights from competitor activities. This includes enhancing conflict resolution skills and communication skills within your team.
Tailor your business strategy to meet the evolving needs of your customer base and prospective customers.
4. Drive Sales and Market Share:
Equip your sales teams with sales battlecards that highlight counters to competitor claims. This helps in armed sales teams making effective pitches to potential customers.
Use market share analysis and market behavior insights to identify areas for growth and achieve double-digit sales gains.

Implement a continuous monitoring approach to stay updated on competitor activities and market shifts. Regularly update your competitive monitoring approach to adapt to changes in the competitive business landscape.

Gaining competitive insights is not a one-time activity but an ongoing process that involves continuous monitoring and adaptation. By understanding your competitors and the broader market dynamics, you can make strategic decisions that enhance your market presence and drive business success.
Use competitive intelligence to inform your strategic planning and business strategy.
Focus on meaningful insights that offer a clear understanding of the competitive landscape.
Regularly update your competitive analysis to stay ahead of market leaders and respond to market shifts.

Turning That Frustrating 'Stuck' Feeling Into Growth
Elevate Your Brand with "A.M.P.: Amplify Your Growth and Re-Energize Your Business"
Innovative Strategies For Revitalizing Stagnant Brands

content.text: In the fast-paced American consumer market, staying relevant is key. Even the most established brands can find their growth plateauing. But with innovative strategies, these brands can be revitalized, paving the way for renewed growth and success.
Understanding Stagnation And Revitalization
Several factors can contribute to a brand becoming stagnant. These can include:

â¢ Market saturation: As a market becomes more saturated, it becomes increasingly difficult for brands to stand out. This can lead to brands becoming complacent and failing to innovate, which can ultimately lead to stagnation.

â¢ Outdated marketing strategies: Brands that rely on outdated marketing strategies are also at risk of becoming stagnant. This is because the way consumers interact with brands has changed dramatically in recent years, and brands that fail to adapt their marketing strategies to reflect these changes will quickly fall behind.

â¢ Loss of touch with evolving customer preferences: Brands that lose touch with evolving customer preferences are also at risk of becoming stagnant. This is because consumers are constantly changing their needs and wants, and brands that fail to keep up will eventually be left behind.
Revitalizing A Stagnant Brand

I've noticed most brands focus a lot on distribution and sales and forget about the importance of brand building and marketing. Revitalizing a brand often involves embracing digital transformation and adopting a customer-centric approach. Enhancing online presence, using data analytics for decision-making, and engaging customers with personalized marketing are key.

Revitalizing a stagnant brand requires a comprehensive approach that addresses the underlying causes of the stagnation. Here are some examples of actions to take to become more relevant and successful in the marketplace:
1. Reevaluate the brand's identity.
To revitalize a stagnant brand, first take a step back and reevaluate the brand's identity. This includes defining the brand's purpose, values and target audience.

For example, my company worked with a juice brand from Brooklyn that saw a decline in sales. To revitalize this brand, we repositioned it to resonate with hip-hop culture, integral to Brooklyn. We integrated themes like rap, basketball and skateboarding into their communications, tapping into the nostalgia for the '80s and '90s.
2. Align with current market needs.

Once the brand's identity has been reestablished, it is important to align the brand with current market needs. This can be done by conducting market research to identify the needs and wants of target consumers.
3. Be innovative with your marketing strategies.

Brands that want to revitalize must also be willing to innovate in their marketing strategies. This may involve using new channels, developing new creative content or experimenting with new ways to reach consumers. It can also include rebranding and speaking to new potential audiences.
The aim is to create excitement around the brand, making it feel fresh and relevant, and creating added value that will result in increased sales and consumer loyalty.

For example, when working with another clientâa family business specializing in Latin cooking ingredients that had never ventured into structured marketing beforeâwe initiated their digital journey with the launch of their first website and social media presence, supported by influencer partnerships. Digital engagement through email marketing, sharing recipes and product use ideas, and launching an Amazon storefront helped expand the company's reach and sales over their original distribution geographic reach.
4. Invest in customer experience.

I've found that the brands that focus on providing a positive customer experience are more likely to retain customers and attract new ones. This can be done by improving customer service, providing easy-to-use products and services, and creating a positive brand image.
5. Build relationships with influencers.

Influencers can be a powerful tool for reaching new audiences and generating awareness for a brand. Brands can build relationships with influencers by providing them with products to review. You can also invite influencers to events or simply start with reaching out to them on social media.
6. Give back to the community.

Brands that give back to the community are more likely to be seen as positive and responsible by consumers. This can be done by supporting local charities, volunteering in the community or simply donating products or services. The juice brand we worked with sponsored a local hip hop festival, which helped them reconnect with Gen Y and Z consumers.

Revitalizing a stagnant brand is about finding the balance between staying true to its roots and evolving with changing market dynamics. With innovative strategies and a keen understanding of market trends and consumer behavior, stagnant brands can not only recover but thrive.

title: Introducing Maison SR by Samba Rock
My Journey with 10,000 Small Businesses
Your Startup Is No Longer Just Starting Up: What's Next?

content.text: You've set the foundation of your business, witnessed the initial outcomes of your vision and now stand at a pivotal juncture where your startup is no longer just starting up. It's an exhilarating phase where the next steps could propel you toward remarkable success. Here are seven lessons to help guide you through this critical growth phase.
1. Dive deep market into research.

Growth is more than scaling up; it's about aligning with the ever-evolving market landscape. Thorough market research is your compass here. You'll need to go beyond demographics and trends and delve into the psyche of your potential customers. Understand their needs, behaviors and preferences; this can illuminate the path for your product development, marketing efforts and customer engagement strategies.
2. Craft a brand that echoes with your audience.
A strong brand narrative can be your beacon in a market flooded with choices. It's imperative to find your differentiator, what you offer that is unique in the market, and capitalize on it.

Tell your story in a way that resonates with your audience to create a lasting emotional connection. People connect with stories that are true and heartfelt. Share your brand's journey, including the ups and downs, to build a sense of trust and connection.

Additionally, keep in mind that your content should be more than just product promotion. It should reflect the values and ethos of your brand. Ensure there is consistency in your messaging, visual identity and values.
4. Explore social media and influencer collaborations.

In the digital age, social media isn't just a marketing tool; it's a dialogue platform. It's where you listen to, respond to and connect with your community. Each social platform has its own flavor and audience with specific expectations.
You can also consider collaborating with influencers whose ethos align with yours; this can further deepen your brand's reach and authenticity.
5. Consider harnessing AI for smarter growth.
Incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) isn't just about staying on trend; it's about leveraging technology to gain insights, improve efficiency and enhance customer experiences.
6. Forge the right partnerships.

One of the most crucial aspects of growing a startup is aligning with the right partners. Strategic partnerships can open new avenues, offer valuable resources and provide market insights that are otherwise hard to access.
7. Foster human connection.

Innovation is key, but you can never forget the human aspect. In a world increasingly driven by digital interactions, never underestimate the human value. Personalized service, genuine interactions and a focus on customer satisfaction can elevate your brand's appeal and foster a loyal community.

As you navigate growing your startup, remember that it's a journey of exploration and learning. Nobody knows everything, and an important part of scaling your business is delegating work so you can focus on your top-level business strategy.

title: Navigating The American Dream: A Guide For International Brands

content.text: The vast landscape of the American market has always shimmered with potential and beckoned emerging brands to experience its rich diversity and opportunities. Yet, like any frontier, its complexity demands more than just ambition; it requires collective strength, strategic vision and an understanding of its intricate tapestry.

My marketing agency helps international brands expand their presence in the U.S., so I've seen firsthand that America is not just a single market. It's a world within itselfâa conglomerate of multifaceted consumers with varied tastes, cultures and demands.
Strategic Expansion Essentials

This is the compass that can help your brand identify current consumer trends, competitive niches and untapped opportunities. International brands should prioritize understanding consumer demographics and psychographics to tap into specific audience needs and buying habits.
4. Direct Consumer Engagement

Establishing bonds with your customers that transcend mere transactions is a conduit to understanding the pulse of the market. Social media channels, especially Instagram, X (formerly known as Twitter) and Facebook provide avenues for real-time interactions.
Building Connections: Networking And Collaborations
Emerging brands often face numerous challenges, including regulatory compliance and intense competition. Building resilience and fostering innovation are essential in navigating these challenges.

I believe forming strategic partnerships and alliances can amplify a brand's reach and impact. Network with industry experts, influencers and other brands to open doors to collaborative opportunities, shared knowledge and mutual growth.

The allure of the American market is undeniable, but the path is strewn with intricacies. However, I believe that when brands keep a few best practices in mind and unite to pool resources and wisdom, they don't just navigate the challengesâthey redefine success.

title: Translating Chess Tactics Into Entrepreneurial Triumphs

content.text: Chess and entrepreneurship might seem worlds apart at first glance. However, as someone who revels in the challenges of both, I assure you that they are closer to each other than they appear. Chess, with its meticulous strategies and calculated maneuvers, can offer entrepreneurs an array of insights that can help them navigate the entrepreneurial journey.

I'm a big fan of chess, and I have played it online every day for years now. With every new game, I notice some connections that apply to my daily life as a business owner, so I decided to share these thoughts with you, my fellow entrepreneur.
1. Choreographing The Dance Between Strategy And Tactics
In the captivating game of chess, long-term strategies seamlessly blend with short-term tactics. This perfect blend of strategy and tactics is just as vital in the business landscape.
2. Embracing Adaptability: The Fluidity Of Chess And Business
A chessboard is a landscape of constant change: a place where flexibility and adaptability reign supreme. The entrepreneurial world is no different.
3. Rivalry As A Source Of Insight And Innovation

Understanding your opponent's strategy is paramount in chess. Keeping tabs on your competitors, anticipating their next moves and learning from their tactics is equally essential in the world of business.
4. Weighing The Scales: The Balance Of Risk And Reward
Chess is a game of calculated risks; sometimes, you might find yourself sacrificing your queen to gain a superior position on the board. The world of business plays the same game of risk and reward.
5. Resilience: The Key To Recovery And Reinvention

In chess, losing a queen isn't the end. It is an opportunity to regroup, reassess the board and approach the game with fresh insight. This resilient mindset is a vital tool in the entrepreneurial world.
6. The Power Of Innovation: Disrupting The Board
Bold, innovative moves in chess can throw off an opponent and disrupt their strategy. Businesses need to channel the same spirit of innovation to stand out from the competition.
7. Commanding Attention: The Power Of Positioning
The power of a chess piece directly correlates with its position on the board. Similarly, a company's market positioning significantly influences its success.
8. Mastery Over Time: The Chess Clock Analogy

In a game of chess, effective time management can be the difference between victory and defeat. In business, managing product development timelines, responding swiftly to market changes and making timely decisions can be a decisive factor.
9. The Game-Changing Potential Of Resources
Every chess piece, from the humble pawn to the mighty queen, has a significant role to play. Similarly, in businesses, each team member brings unique skills and potential that can change the game.
10. Striking The Perfect Balance: Offense And Defense
Chess players know when to go on the offensive and when to protect their king. Entrepreneurs, too, need to strike a balance between growth strategies and defending market share and resources.

In sum, the intricate game of chess offers an untapped wealth of insights for entrepreneurs. By viewing our businesses through the lens of a chess grandmaster, we can navigate the complex world of entrepreneurship with clarity and foresight.

title: Navigating the Social Media Dilemma: Agency vs Internal Team
The Art of Going Global: Lessons on Building a Strong International Brand
Thinking Outside The Borders: Brazilian E-Commerce Opportunities

content.text: With the rise of digital commerce reshaping global markets, I see many U.S. brands on the hunt for the next frontier. As it turns out, this frontier might be nestled in Brazil's energetic streets and vibrant online market.

I was born in Brazil and arrived in the U.S. in 2009. Although I became an American citizen in 2018, I never left my Brazilian roots. In 2015, I started a boutique agency whose mission is to help Brazilian brands to expand into the U.S. market.
Brazil: A Thriving E-Commerce Hub

Brazil, known for its sprawling landscapes and diverse culture, is also home to a fast-paced e-commerce market. Brazil's online sales reached roughly $18 billion in 2020, reflecting a staggering growth of 41% from the previous year.

I've found that a significant chunk of Brazilian consumers display a strong desire for international brands, with 68% of online shoppers in Brazil saying they have bought from international merchantsâwith the U.S. being a popular choice.
Brazilians' Affinity For American Products
The propensity for American products isn't limited to online purchases. Brazilians, when traveling to the U.S., consider shopping a primary activity.
Cross-Border Logistics Simplified
One of the challenges of selling products internationally is international shipping. This is where cross-border logistics comes in.
When looking to break into Brazil's e-commerce market, I recommend utilizing cross-border logistics since this removes the need to have a warehouse in Brazil.
The Importance Of Local Expertise
But making a splash in Brazil takes more than just opening an online store. It's important to understand the Brazilian market and how to connect with local shoppers.

There's no doubt that Brazil's online shopping scene offers a golden opportunity for U.S. brands. But to truly unlock this potential, I believe that you need to understand the market and find the right partners to guide you.
Entering a new market is a big step. Always do your homework, learn the market and get expert advice before making your move. You will say "Obrigado" later.

title: The Power of Localization: Strategies for Adapting Your Brand to International Markets
Making Lemonade: Turning A Layoff Into An Opportunity

content.text: Many people say Easter is about new life, a time of significant life changes. That's what happened to me in 2015. I was working at an advertising group, for the agency's top client. All the employees working on that account were invited to a meeting. We were informed that our client was consolidating their business with a single agency group, and they decided to go with one of our competitors.

We all went home early that day. The first thing I did was open my laptop, go online and start my business. I had this idea of starting a boutique marketing agency, but I never had the courage to take the leap of faith.
But now, life decided to give me "a little push," and I had 30 days to make it happen while still employed or to try to find another job.

Easter came, and the next day, my life changed. I was laid off for the first time, along with my colleagues. It was bittersweetâI was ready to do my own thing, but I hated that it wasn't my own decision to leave. However, it was also the best thing that could happen to my career.
So that moment when I got laid off for the first time, as horrible as it may sound, was actually a turning point in my career. Life gave me lemons, and I knew exactly what to do with them.
I had the idea long before the layoff and never had the courage to execute it. Once I needed it, I had a business plan ready and a chance to bring it to life. Always be prepared.
I suggest keeping your ideas in writing, a simplified version of a business plan. No need to go too deep into the details since you will learn a lot once you start executing it.
3. Cultivate great connections.
My friends, colleagues and connections all helped me find the clients I needed when I was prospecting, especially during that first month, but also throughout the past 8 years.
4. Embrace the learning curve.

I recognized that I had a lot to learn in order to manage my own business, from finances to administration and HR. Instead of shying away from these challenges, I embraced the learning process and acquired new skills.
By following these steps, I managed to turn a potentially devastating life event into a new beginning and a fulfilling career. Remember, when life gives you lemons, making the lemonade is up to you.

title: Unlocking The Power Of AI In Marketing And Business Development

content.text: Artificial intelligence (AI) is a powerful tool that has the potential to revolutionize the way businesses operate and interact with their customers. While some may fear that AI will replace human labor and creativity, I see the potential for it to be a powerful ally in improving efficiency, accuracy and personalization.

Throughout my 25-year career in marketing, I have consistently integrated new technologies into my daily routine. As the owner of a boutique agency, I rely on various tools to optimize my team's time and make our clients' expansion projects feasible.
AI-powered analytics tools have the potential to improve targeting, messaging and campaign effectiveness by quickly and accurately analyzing vast amounts of data.
Analyzing and Organizing Data

Like in other business applications, AI-powered analytics tools can help you quickly and accurately analyze vast amounts of data, allowing you to make more informed decisions about targeting, messaging and campaigns.
AI can also assist with personalization by using data and algorithms to deliver customized experiences and content to individual customers.
Another area where AI can be useful is in automating routine or time-consuming tasks, such as email marketing, social media scheduling and lead generation.
Ethical And Legal Considerations For AI
However, it's important to approach AI with caution and skepticism. Technology is still evolving rapidly, and there are many ethical and legal considerations to take into account when using it.
Transparency is crucial, with clear communication about how AI is being used and data protection. Focusing on diversity and inclusion is also essential to avoid bias in AI implementation.
Balance Between Automation And Creativity

I find that the key to success with AI is to strike the right balance between automation and creativity. AI can help to automate routine tasks and provide valuable data insights, but it should not replace the human touch in marketing.

AI creates based on an average of its input, resulting in copy that sounds similar and lacks the brand's unique identity. While AI can assist in organizing ideas, human copywriters are still necessary to inject personality and relatability into creative work.
By striking the right balance between automation and creativity, marketing teams can gain a competitive edge and drive success in an increasingly complex and challenging business world.

EstadÃ£o, the largest newspaper in Brazil (it's like that country's The New York Times for comparison purposes) has just published an article about internationalization and marketing based on an interview given by our founder and CEO Valter Klug (who was quoted throughout the article) to the renowned journalist Paulo Silvestre! Here is the translated version of the article (the original is available here):
Internationalizing a brand brings benefits but requires caution
By Paulo Fernando Silvestre Jr.

Everyone knows what panettone is, right? That may be true in Brazil, but not in the United States, where this traditional cake is not part of the Christmas menu. Therefore, when Bauducco decided to enter the US market, it couldn't simply advertise its product: it needed to explain it to the population.

This is a typical challenge in the process of brand internationalization. In a globalized world, many companies try to replicate their success in other markets. However, formulas that work well in one country may not work in others.

"In 2014, nobody knew what panettone was in the United States, so we had to develop the category, make it known and appeal to Americans," explains Valter Klug, CEO of Samba Rock agency, specializing in international brand expansion. "It was a much bigger challenge than simply communicating a brand in another market."

Operating in other countries can bring many benefits, such as opening new markets for the brand, reducing dependence on a single market, protecting the business from local economic instability, and gaining reputation and experience. Furthermore, pioneering a category in a new market can be attractive, as the brand can dominate it for a while, consolidating its image and selling without competition.

However, internationalization is not for everyone. Even an established and successful brand in its home country needs planning to enter a new market. It's a long-term investment, not suitable for adventurers.

"The vast majority of brands that go to the United States end up breaking in the first year because they didn't plan properly or because there was no market for them," explains Klug. "Sometimes you even have to adapt your product."

Aligning expectations is also essential, as managers need to understand that they will be virtually startups in a new market. Maturation of the business and return on initial investment may take months or even years.

Having someone with in-depth knowledge of the new market is crucial in the internationalization process. Working together with the company's team, these professionals can make adjustments to offers, creating a consistent global communication strategy. Being aware of local peculiarities can make the difference between success and failure.

The American market is often the preferred choice for expansion due to its size, opportunities, lower taxes, and less bureaucracy. However, personal experience as a tourist is not enough for business success. This reinforces the importance of support from professionals experienced in local peculiarities.

Another example of cultural differences is the use of WhatsApp for customer relationships. Widely used in Brazil, the messaging app is less popular in the US, where customers prefer Facebook Messenger or even SMS. Not considering this difference can lead to serious relationship problems with the new audience.

"It's not enough to have a good product; you need to know the market, study it, and create a business plan to support the project's viability," concludes Klug. "It's not just about translating what works in Brazil."

title: The Importance of Communication in Brand Internationalization

content.text: 0 Skip to Content @supports (-webkit-backdrop-filter: none) or (backdrop-filter: none) { .header-blur-background { } } MENU ð§ð· HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ MENU ðºð¸ HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call .top-bun, .patty, .bottom-bun { height: 1px; } Open Menu Close Menu .top-bun, .patty, .bottom-bun { height: 1px; } Open Menu Close Menu MENU ð§ð· HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ MENU ðºð¸ HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call Folder: MENU ð§ð· Back HOME 1: Soundcheck 2: Pesquisa & EstratÃ©gia 3: Posicionamento & Plano 4: Samba+ Folder: MENU ðºð¸ Back HOME CPG AMP TROPICALIA REBRAND NEWS Book a free call The Importance of Communication in Brand Internationalization Mar 6 Written By Valter Klug Originally published in Portuguese on Mundo do Marketing website

Each region's language is unique. It may seem obvious, but not every Portuguese-speaking country uses the same expressions, slang, and even some words have different meanings when used in different contexts. The same goes for English, Spanish, and other languages. So why do some companies think translation is all communication takes?

Considering a global communication strategy is essential for brand internationalization. Understanding the peculiarities of each location can make the difference between success or being ignored. I know several stories of people who embarked with the confidence that "my product will sell anywhere" and had a rather unpleasant surprise. There are ads on large platforms, like Amazon, with incorrect text in the description; products advertised without proper campaigns; campaigns with completely inappropriate slogans for a particular country; and social media pages hastily translated from the main language without the necessary adaptation.

Relying on some "certainties" can be disastrous. Who would have thought (in Brazil) that using a simple "vocÃª" in a campaign in Portugal could be offensive? Or that WhatsApp is not as popular in other countries as it is in Brazil? A campaign through the app in the United States, for example, could be a huge failure, as Americans hardly use it. Text messages are sent via SMS. These and other peculiarities must be considered.

Another common trend I often see is hiring an agency at the beginning, creating a communication plan, "learning the day-to-day," then dismissing the agency and relying on an internal team member (probably a junior) to execute actions (especially on social media, because who doesn't know how nowadays, right?). This is when problems begin, and usually, that same client returns to the agency sometime later.

The international market, especially the United States, is not for beginners. I say this without any disrespect, but to emphasize that an in-person presence is necessary for the brand to evolve. A specialized agency in the process genuinely makes a difference. It is a long-term construction that evolves faster with someone who knows the chosen region.

An agency may not know the product as well as those who live with it daily, but it has multidisciplinary professionals who will combine their expertise gained from working with other clients to create the best strategies for that brand. Years of study, constant updates, and active monitoring of market developments in various sectors make up a vast knowledge bank. They may not know all the answers to specific questions, but by working in synergy with the company's marketing team, they will come up with the best options for each scenario.

The chances of success increase when finding an agency with the right profile for the company. In a boutique format, for example, more personalized service is possible. In addition, to experience in the market, you intend to expand to, knowing the Brazilian market is also essential to convey the product's unique features in the best way. This closer service offers other benefits: faster approvals, customization, value adjustment, and a stronger connection with the client.

Investing in the international market is attractive, offering various commercial benefits and enhancing brand reputation. However, it can lead to the opposite of what was intended without the proper strategy. Partnering with an agency that conducts market research, offers opinions on the business, identifies opportunities, and has successful cases in the region is a good indicator. Choose carefully, communicate assertively, and achieve results in dollars!

*Valter Klug is the CEO and founder of Samba Rock, an integrated advertising and marketing agency specializing in helping major brands strategically, creatively, and efficiently expand globally. With over 20 years of experience in integrated communication, interactive design, and social media, Valter has worked in South America, Europe, and North America, in three of the most internationally awarded countries in advertising (Brazil, United Kingdom, and the United States). He has worked with four of the largest advertising groups in the industry (WPP/Y&R, Isobar, Euro RSCG/HAVAS, and SapientNitro), with brands such as AT&T, ESPN, Coca-Cola, Pepsico, Microsoft, Nokia, Sony, ItaÃº, AMBEV, Banco Inter, and founded Samba Rock in Miami (USA) in 2015.

International expansion is an increasingly sought-after strategy for brands. The digital age has amplified the effects of globalization, and the opening of the global market is an attractive opportunity for growth. However, what may seem simple at first - "Isn't it just about selling online and leveraging marketplaces?" - requires research and planning.

To achieve a significant position in the international arena, it is crucial to reassess marketing strategies and adopt a different approach to attract consumers unfamiliar with the products. The competition also increases exponentially, as products or services must compete with brands from other countries already established in their respective markets.

Having spent 25 years working in integrated communication with global experience (in South America, Europe, and North America), I can assert that developing a customized strategy is essential to achieving the desired results. Here are 10 actions that should not be overlooked in the internationalization process:
1 - Conduct market research

It's vital to have a thorough understanding of the target market, its needs, and preferences. This includes competitive analysis, consumer trends, and cultural differences. Research what competitors are doing, understand their positioning, and identify your brand's competitive edge.
2 - Develop a customized marketing strategy

The marketing strategy must be tailored to the target market, considering the brand's competitive advantages and the audience's unique characteristics. Does your product already exist in the country? What is its acceptance in the market? How do current consumers behave? What are the growth opportunities? How does your product compare to the main players in the market?
3 - Regionalize the brand

Merely translating is not enough: you must adapt the message, including tone of voice, visual identity, and persona, to appeal to the new target market. It's also essential to evaluate if the brand name is pronounceable in the target country or if rebranding is necessary. For instance, "A Tal da Castanha," a brand from 3 CoraÃ§Ãµes, underwent rebranding as "PlantCo" for global markets during its international expansion.
4 - Establish partnerships and network
Building relationships with local companies and organizations helps to increase brand visibility and credibility in the new market.
5 - Identify and use ideal distribution channels

It's crucial to select the most suitable channels for distributing products or services in the new location. These may differ from those used in the home country, highlighting the importance of prior research, taking all variables into account.
6 - Create a website and strengthen digital presence

Simply translating the website and social media profiles into the local language is not enough; cultural adaptation is necessary. Literal translations can lead to misinterpretation, nonsensical expressions, and inaccurate messaging. Incorrect translations can even compromise a company's reputation.
7 - Address legal and regulatory issues

Ensure the brand complies with all laws and regulations in the target country. For example, when expanding to the U.S., it's recommended to register the brand with the USPTO (United States Patent and Trademark Office).
8 - Create a regional team

Hire employees or partners to help the brand navigate the new market and communicate effectively with the target audience. Having professionals with years of experience in the country is key to achieving the perfect match.

Advertising, public relations, and other marketing strategies tailored to the new region make a difference. With GuaranÃ¡ Antarctica, for example, we aimed to connect the American audience with two Brazilian passions (soccer and GuaranÃ¡), which are increasingly popular in the U.S. We identified that NYCFC (New York City Football Club) was the only Major League Soccer team without a soda brand sponsorship and seized the opportunity as a relationship-building platform, promoting various activities with their players and fans.
10 - Be patient and persistent
Success in a new country may take time, so it's important to have a long-term perspective and be willing to adapt and change plans as necessary.
Promoting a successful brand from Brazil in a country unfamiliar with it will only be feasible if the brand's market stage and the desired market's maturity are clear. Study, be inspired, and invest!

*Valter Klug is the CEO and founder of Samba Rock, an integrated advertising and marketing agency specializing in helping major brands strategically, creatively, and efficiently expand globally. With over 20 years of experience in integrated communication, interactive design, and social media, Valter has worked in South America, Europe, and North America, in three of the most internationally awarded countries in advertising (Brazil, United Kingdom, and the United States). He has worked with four of the largest advertising groups in the industry (WPP/Y&R, Isobar, Euro RSCG/HAVAS, and SapientNitro), with brands such as AT&T, ESPN, Coca-Cola, Pepsico, Microsoft, Nokia, Sony, ItaÃº, AMBEV, Banco Inter, and founded Samba Rock in Miami (USA) in 2015.
